title: Reuters: Soaring AI bills are pushing companies toward cheaper models
friendlyTitle: Companies are choosing cheaper AI models
summary: Reuters reports that businesses are moving more workloads from premium frontier models to cheaper, smaller, open-source, and Chinese models as usage-based AI costs become harder to predict. The article cites OpenRouter data showing open-source token share rising sharply and companies adopting routing strategies to reserve expensive models for harder tasks.
friendlySummary: Many businesses are finding that AI bills can rise very quickly. They are starting to use cheaper models for simple tasks and save the expensive models for harder work.
whyItMatters: This is the clearest sign yet that the everyday AI stack is becoming a cost-optimization problem, not just a capability race. For teams using AI heavily, model routing, budgets, and task-level model choice are becoming basic operational skills.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If you use AI at work, the best tool may not always be the most powerful one. Picking the right model for the job can save a lot of money.
